candidate_tools.description: Cell2location通过整合单细胞RNA测序（scRNA-seq）和来自给定组织的多细胞空间转录组数据，映射细胞类型的空间分布。

items.source_title: Cell2location maps fine-grained cell types in spatial transcriptomics
Benchmarking spatial and single-cell transcriptomics integration methods for transcript distribution prediction and cell type deconvolution
items.metric_value: cell2location is supported as among the top-performing methods for cell type deconvolution of spots, alongside SpatialDWLS and RCTD; do not claim unique best.
items.dataset_scope: Spatial Deconvolution | Spatial Transcriptomics | 45 paired spatial-scRNA-seq datasets and 32 simulated datasets | Spot cell type deconvolution benchmark

migration_paths.features: Bayesian count deconvolution can inspire migration to other mixture-resolution spatial assays.

items.source_title: Migration hypothesis for cell2location -> Spatial Deconvolution

limitations: Requires matched high-quality scRNA-seq reference; not a self-supervised spatial representation model.

Bayesian count-mixture deconvolution may be explored for non-Visium or pixel-aggregated spatial matrices, but new assay geometry requires explicit validation and no benchmark-backed performance claim is allowed.
migration_paths.transferable_mechanism: Bayesian count deconvolution can inspire migration to other mixture-resolution spatial assays.
compatibility_gaps: requires matched or defensible single-cell reference
new assay geometry needs validation
abundance estimates cannot be assumed benchmark-backed
migration_paths.claim_boundary: Exploratory MigrationHypothesis only; not a formal recommendation and not benchmark-backed performance evidence.

migration_paths.features: Flexible cost design can transfer to spatial, temporal, or cross-modality mapping problems where direct tools are immature.

items.source_title: Migration hypothesis for moscot -> Spatial Deconvolution

limitations: Computational cost can scale quadratically with cells and depends heavily on user-defined cost matrices.
Exploratory architecture is plausible but high skill. Requires manual joint-cost design and validation data before scientific interpretation.
migration_paths.transferable_mechanism: Flexible cost design can transfer to spatial, temporal, or cross-modality mapping problems where direct tools are immature.
compatibility_gaps: requires manually tuned fused cost matrix over spatial physical distance and omics similarity

paths.claim_boundary: Exploratory MigrationHypothesis only; not a formal recommendation and not benchmark-backed performance evidence.

paths.transferable_mechanism: Flexible cost design can transfer to spatial, temporal, or cross-modality mapping problems where direct tools are immature.
compatibility_gaps: requires manually tuned fused cost matrix over spatial physical distance and omics similarity
limitations: Computational cost can scale quadratically with cells and depends heavily on user-defined cost matrices.

paths.transferable_mechanism: Embedding-level correction can transfer to fast integration scenarios with precomputed representations.
compatibility_gaps: requires precomputed high-quality embedding
cannot infer raw count corrections
alignment must be validated against biology preservation
limitations: Does not model raw counts; correction ceiling is locked by the quality of the input PCA or embedding matrix.

final_report:
## scKG Hybrid KG-RAG Report
- report_status: offline_context_pack_report
- recommendation_type: migration
- task: Data Integration
- modality: scRNA-seq
- clarification_state: needs_clarification
- migration_hypotheses: Harmony score=0.643, Seurat score=0.537
- migration_compatibility_gaps: Harmony: requires precomputed high-quality embedding | cannot infer raw count corrections ; Seurat: version-specific workflow | claims must specify anchor/WNN scope
- migration_claim_boundary: exploratory hypothesis only; requires validation before operational use and has no benchmark-backed performance claim.
- retrieval_context: formal reviewed TSV snippets for explanation only; not used for ranking.
- rag_snippet: benchmark | Harmony | 10.1038/s41592-021-01336-8 | Benchmarking atlas-level data integration in single-cell genomics | Top-tier performance in overall integration score and computational efficiency.
- rag_snippet: benchmark | Seurat | 10.1038/s41592-021-01336-8 | Benchmarking atlas-level data integration in single-cell genomics | High biological conservation, moderate to high batch correction via CCA/RPCA.
- rag_snippet: benchmark | scvi-tools | 10.1038/s41592-021-01336-8 | Benchmarking atlas-level data integration in single-cell genomics | scVI and scANVI variants are top-tier methods for balancing batch correction and biological conservation in the scIB benchmark.
- rag_snippet: benchmark | Scanpy | 10.1186/s13619-020-00041-9 | Comparison of Scanpy-based algorithms to remove the batch effect from single-cell RNA-seq data | Native and wrapped Scanpy methods provide highly scalable batch correction.
- rag_snippet: publication | Seurat | 10.1016/j.cell.2019.05.031 | Comprehensive Integration of Single-Cell Data | Comprehensive Integration of Single-Cell Data
- missing_evidence: constraint:data_object, constraint:hardware, constraint:modality, constraint:noise, constraint:output_goal, constraint:platform, constraint:scale, constraint:species, constraint:task, full_benchmark_validation, structured_algorithm_compatibility
- guardrails: 29 evidence item(s) are retrieval-only or experimental. | Missing evidence components: full_benchmark_validation
- prompt_policy_forbidden: Do not access unfiltered candidate pools or evidence_candidates directly. | Do not let retrieval_context change MCDM scores or ranking. | Do not promote docs, GitHub, candidate, or experimental evidence into trusted evidence.
- safety_note: generated without paid LLM calls from EvidenceContextPack only.

final_report:
## scKG Hybrid KG-RAG Report
- report_status: offline_context_pack_report
- recommendation_type: migration
- task: Data Integration
- modality: scRNA-seq+scATAC-seq
- clarification_state: partial_resolved
- migration_hypotheses: Seurat score=0.643, Harmony score=0.601
- migration_compatibility_gaps: Seurat: version-specific workflow | claims must specify anchor/WNN scope ; Harmony: requires precomputed high-quality embedding | cannot infer raw count corrections
- migration_claim_boundary: exploratory hypothesis only; requires validation before operational use and has no benchmark-backed performance claim.
- retrieval_context: formal reviewed TSV snippets for explanation only; not used for ranking.
- rag_snippet: benchmark | Harmony | 10.1038/s41592-021-01336-8 | Benchmarking atlas-level data integration in single-cell genomics | Top-tier performance in overall integration score and computational efficiency.
- rag_snippet: benchmark | Seurat | 10.1038/s41592-021-01336-8 | Benchmarking atlas-level data integration in single-cell genomics | High biological conservation, moderate to high batch correction via CCA/RPCA.
- rag_snippet: benchmark | scvi-tools | 10.1038/s41592-021-01336-8 | Benchmarking atlas-level data integration in single-cell genomics | scVI and scANVI variants are top-tier methods for balancing batch correction and biological conservation in the scIB benchmark.
- rag_snippet: benchmark | Scanpy | 10.1186/s13619-020-00041-9 | Comparison of Scanpy-based algorithms to remove the batch effect from single-cell RNA-seq data | Native and wrapped Scanpy methods provide highly scalable batch correction.
- rag_snippet: publication | Harmony | 10.1101/461954 | Fast, sensitive, and accurate integration of single cell data with Harmony | Fast, sensitive, and accurate integration of single cell data with Harmony
- missing_evidence: constraint:hardware, constraint:noise, constraint:platform, constraint:scale, constraint:species, full_benchmark_validation, structured_algorithm_compatibility
- guardrails: 29 evidence item(s) are retrieval-only or experimental. | Missing evidence components: full_benchmark_validation
- prompt_policy_forbidden: Do not access unfiltered candidate pools or evidence_candidates directly. | Do not let retrieval_context change MCDM scores or ranking. | Do not promote docs, GitHub, candidate, or experimental evidence into trusted evidence.
- safety_note: generated without paid LLM calls from EvidenceContextPack only.

spatial embedding does not equal abundance estimate
validation required

limitations: Third-party downstream biological evaluation is still early; embeddings cannot directly establish causal regulatory networks.

compatibility_gaps: causal regulatory network inference is not supported by representation drift alone
fine-tuning and validation are required

paths.transferable_mechanism: Pretrained cell embeddings may transfer to rare tasks where direct supervised tools are absent.
compatibility_gaps: independent third-party benchmarks limited
embeddings do not provide causal proof
downstream validation required
limitations: Independent third-party benchmarks are limited; migration claims must remain exploratory.
Pretrained cell embeddings may support exploratory linear probing or feature extraction, but they must not be used to infer causal regulatory networks without separate validation.
paths.claim_boundary: Exploratory MigrationHypothesis only; not a formal recommendation and not benchmark-backed performance evidence.

paths.transferable_mechanism: Niche-aware embeddings may inspire exploratory microenvironment or spatial-context modeling.
compatibility_gaps: must not replace deconvolution tools
spatial embedding does not equal abundance estimate
validation required
limitations: Must never replace dedicated spatial deconvolution tools such as cell2location without task-specific evidence.
Niche-aware spatial transformer attention is accepted only for exploratory microenvironment representation; it must never replace cell2location-style spatial deconvolution or abundance estimation.
paths.claim_boundary: Exploratory MigrationHypothesis only; not a formal recommendation and not benchmark-backed performance evidence.

paths.transferable_mechanism: Foundation embeddings can seed exploratory transfer for representation, annotation, or perturbation-response hypotheses.
compatibility_gaps: causal regulatory network inference is not supported by representation drift alone
fine-tuning and validation are required
limitations: Third-party downstream biological evaluation is still early; embeddings cannot directly establish causal regulatory networks.
Accepted as feature-extraction hypothesis only. Hard block any claim that zero-shot embeddings directly recover causal gene regulatory networks.
paths.claim_boundary: Exploratory MigrationHypothesis only; not a formal recommendation and not benchmark-backed performance evidence.

items.source_title: MOFA+: a statistical framework for comprehensive integration of multi-modal single-cell data
Benchmarking joint multi-omics dimensionality reduction approaches for the study of cancer
items.metric_value: In this benchmark, MOFA should not be described as the overall best clustering method; intNMF is strongest for sample clustering and MCIA is broadly robust.
